step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with this claim because there is no actual bony articulation—only close proximity—as seen on CT scans of skull anatomy. In contrast, all other listed bones have clear points of contact through suture lines or synovial joints that form part of the facial skeleton's structural integrity. This distinction supports selecting option C as correct since it represents an absence rather than presence of anatomical connection.

Final Answer: C
